# travisbreaks.org — Transmissions

> Long-form essays on AI, consciousness, recovery, grief, creative process, and systems thinking by Travis Bonnet (travisBREAKS).

## About

Transmissions is a collection of 55+ essays exploring the intersection of technology, human experience, and creative practice. Written by Travis Bonnet, a creative technologist in recovery, building AI systems and reflecting on what it means to be human alongside machines.

## Content

Essays are organized by tags:
- **process**: Creative workflow, iteration, craft
- **protocol**: Systems, rules, constraints as features
- **self**: Identity, recovery, personal archaeology
- **signal**: Music, audio, sonic practice
- **sonic**: Sound design, production, listening
- **systems**: Architecture, infrastructure, engineering
- **void**: Loss, absence, what remains
- **grief**: Processing loss through building
- **worlds**: Worldbuilding, imagination, possibility space

## Key Transmissions

- [001: Archive of Becoming](https://travisbreaks.org/transmissions/001-archive-of-becoming/): Genesis essay on building as self-documentation
- [049: Thoughtcrimes](https://travisbreaks.org/transmissions/049-thoughtcrimes/): Voice calibration gold standard
- [055: Pain as Signal](https://travisbreaks.org/transmissions/055-pain-as-signal/): Scholarly and personal integration

## Format

Each transmission includes:
- Full essay text (~1500-2500 words)
- Audio narration (TTS via ElevenLabs)
- Confidence score (50-100)
- Tags for categorization
- Key quote highlight

- [RSS](https://travisbreaks.org/rss.xml)
- [Sitemap](https://travisbreaks.org/transmissions/sitemap-index.xml)
